characters.name: Agent Z
characters.description: Agent Zis a character in the 2017Wacky Racesseries. Agent Z is a young slender Russian woman with long black hair and blue eyes. She wears a grey fedora with a red strip on it, a grey trench coat with a red collar, a red belt with a yellow buckle, dark grey gloves, dark stockings, and red boots. Her facial structure resembles Penelope Pitstop. As a Spy, Agent Z is skilled in the martial arts, a mistress of disguise, and slaps people (mostly Dastardly) in the face, even when she's tied up.

characters.name: Brick Crashman
characters.description: Brick Crashmanis a character in the 2017Wacky Racesseries.  He was voiced byChristopher Judge. He is the race announcer.

characters.name: Brick Crashman
characters.description: Brick Crashmanis a character in the 2017Wacky Racesseries.  He was voiced byChristopher Judge. He is the race announcer.

characters.name: Desdemona Dastardly
characters.description: Desdemona Dastardlyis a character in the 2017Wacky Racesseries. Desdemona is the Bavarian cousin ofDick Dastardly.  Her head shape and facial appearance are very similar to her cousin Dick's, having the same chin, nose, mustache, and eyes.  Unlike her cousin, however, she has two curved eyelashes on each eye, wears pink lipstick, and has long, thick, blonde hair complete with bangs, the back part tied in a bun and the sides tied in braided pigtails with purple ribbon hairbands.  Her arms are thin, but the rest of her body appears to be obese.  She wears various outfits throughout her lone episode appearance.  She lives over the Alps and has her own pretzel mines.  In "Pretzel Logic", she captures the Racers during a competition over the Alps and locks them in her pretzel mines. TheWacky Races (2017)episode:

characters.name: Dick Dastardly
characters.description: Dick Dastardlyis a character inWacky Races, its spin-offDastardly & Muttley in Their Flying Machines, and various other animated series byHanna-BarberaProductions.  He is essentially a caricature of the English actorTerry-Thomas, and of Professor Fate, a comic villain played byJack Lemmonin the filmThe Great Race.  Dastardly was aided in his schemes by his sidekick, a scruffy dog namedMuttleywho had a distinctive wheezy laugh, heard most often when Dastardly's schemes failed. A perpetual cheater and greedy schemer, Dastardly is always paired with sidekick Muttley. He debuted as the villain ofWacky Races, where his vehicle wasThe Mean Machine(Double Zero). He often resorted to many despicable tricks to win each race, but his schemes always backfired. In the debut episode, Dastardly lands in fourth place as he was taking a pose with his hat off when it shows in replay until the next episodes, he ends up being disqualified in first place and always in last place. He later appeared inDastardly & Muttley in Their Flying Machines, where Dastardly and Muttley led the Vulture Squadron with two other pilots: Zilly, a coward who used to hide in his clothes when he was ordered to deploy, and Klunk, the mechanic/inventor, who speaks a language composed of strange sounds that only Zilly can understand. The squadron constantly attempt to stop a messenger pigeon, Yankee Doodle Pigeon, from delivering messages to an opposing army. As Wacky Races is inspired by the filmThe Great Race, so was Dastardly derived from the film's chief villain, Professor Fate, played by Jack Lemmon. Dastardly in this series wore old-fashioned racer's gear — a long blue overcoat, long red gloves, and a large striped hat with driving goggles attached, and sported a handlebar mustache.

characters.name: Gruesome Twosome
characters.description: The Gruesome Twosome The Gruesome Twosome, driving their Creepy Coupe TheGruesome Twosomeare characters onWacky Races.  Big Gruesome was originally voiced byDaws Butlerand Li'l Gruesome byDon Messick, they are currently voiced by Jim Cummings and Billy West.

characters.name: Hooded Claw
characters.description: Penelope Pitstop, captured by her nemesis, the Hooded Claw Hooded Clawis a character inThe Perils of Penelope Pitstop.  He is the alter ego ofSylvester Sneekly.  He was voiced byPaul Lynde. In the 2017Wacky Racesseries, he was voiced byTom Kennydoing an impression of Paul Lynde. Sylvester Sneekly is the guardian ofPenelope Pitstop, yet he desires her vast fortune she is entitled to. He intends to have her dead so he will become the only remaining claimant of the fortune. Of course to protect his identity, he carries out his plans and traps as a sadistic, sneeky, foul, master of disguise villain known as the Hooded Claw. He does have a foul, childish temper, especially shown when his plans are foiled by Penelope,The Ant Hill Mobor even his own blundering henchmen, the Bully Brothers.

characters.name: I.Q. Ickly
characters.description: Ignatius Quinton "I.Q." Icklyis a boy genius and one of the Wacky Racers in the 2017Wacky Racesseries.  He was voiced byJill Talley. I.Q. is a very intelligent boy of his age often called a "walking encyclopedia" by Dastardly and has little social capability. LikeProfessor Pat Pendingbefore him, he is a genius with a large amount of inventions that he uses throughout the race. As he is only ten years old and he does not actually drive, his car drives itself due to it being a sentient AI.

characters.name: Klunk
characters.description: Klunkis a character inDastardly & Muttley in Their Flying Machines.  He was voiced byDon Messick. Klunk is the Vulture Squadron's stout and steady chief designer and pilot. He is always developing aircraft or weapons with odd features and attachments designed to catchYankee Doodle Pigeon. His weapons are often missiles which home on some bizarre trait, such as a person sneezing. His attempts to explain his inventions are complicated by the fact that he apparently suffers from Tourette syndrome; his speech is punctuated by howls, clicks, whistles, and growls, accompanied by bizarre facial contortions.  OnlyZillycan understand him. His eyes are never seen because of his bangs and goggles pulled over them. Klunk often assumes command inDick Dastardly's absence, like when he went on holiday or suffered from memory loss. Klunk is probably the most consistent character; he is steadfastly committed, beyond all other aspects of his life, to stopping the pigeon.

characters.name: Lazy Luke and Blubber Bear
characters.description: Lazy LukeandBlubber Bearare characters inWacky Races. They were both originally voiced byJohn Stephenson. Lazy Luke is ahillbillywho is half-asleep during most of the races. He drivesThe Arkansas Chug-a-Bugwhile seated on a rocking chair and with his feet perched on the steering wheel. A large but nervous bear named Blubber is his sidekick. Blubber is clad in a scarf and early aviator's helmet with goggles. Blubber, who often bites his nails and shakes, wakes up Luke, who always says: "Blubber, what in tarnation is goin' on here?" or "What in tarnation is that?"

characters.name: Mr. Viceroy
characters.description: Mr. Vice Royis a character inWacky Races Forever. He was voiced byJeff Bennett. He isPeter Perfect's lawyer. But he wants to take over the company with the help of hired villain,Dick Dastardlyand his palMuttley, whom he will hire when he takes over. He would take over if Dick Dastardly wins the race and makes the other racers, even the children of Perfect andPenelope Pitstop,ParkerandPiperlose the race.

characters.name: Mumbly
characters.description: Mumblyis a character inLaff-A-Lympics. He was voiced byDon Messick. He started out in his own animated series,The Mumbly Cartoon Show, which was part ofThe Tom & Jerry Grape Ape Showin 1976. During this time, he was a crime-solving detective modeled after Columbo. A year later, inLaff-A-Lympics, he became the captain of The Really Rottens, a team consisting of originalHanna-Barberavillains.

characters.name: Muttley
characters.description: Wacky Races Dastardly & Muttleyand later series Muttleyis a character inWacky RacesandDastardly & Muttley in Their Flying Machines.  He was created byIwao Takamotoand originally voiced byDon Messick(who also voiced Scooby-Doo).  Since the 2000Wacky Racesvideo game, he was voiced byBilly West.

characters.name: Muttley's girlfriend
characters.description: Muttley's girlfriendis a character inDastardly & Muttley in Their Flying Machines. She appears in some of the "Magnificent Muttley" shorts. She is usually the Damsel in distress whomMuttleyhas to rescue fromDick Dastardly's clutches. She looks like a female of Muttley's breed, but unlike him she is able to speak distinctly. A picture of her hangs on the wall next to Muttley's bed.

characters.description: Pearl Tricia "P.T." Barnstormis a character in the 2017Wacky Racesseries. She was voiced byJill Talley. She is the owner and sponsor of the Wacky Races. She is a short woman with gray hair.

characters.name: Pandora Pitstop
characters.description: Pandora Pitstopis a character in the 2017Wacky Racesseries.  She is voiced byNicole Parker. Pandora is the twin sister ofPenelope Pitstop.  She wears a purple race suit and she has black hair and purple eyes. She grew up in her sister's shadow. On one occasion, Penelope won a talent contest by just taking a step. characters.name: Penelope Pitstop
characters.description: Penelope "Penny" Pitstopis a character inWacky RacesandThe Perils of Penelope Pitstop. She was originally voiced byJanet Waldo. She is the only female racer in theWacky Raceslineup (not that she minds), driving a pink feminine car known asThe Compact Pussycatwhich has personal grooming facilities that would sometimes backfire on other racers, such as shampoo foam hitting their faces. She always has time to relax and worry about her looks, because her car is like a beauty parlor. Penelope Pitstop also has a habit of holding her arm out in the breeze to dry newly applied nail polish, which the others mistake for a turn signal (this habit once sent the Roaring Plenty into a cactus). Peter Perfecthad a crush on her, a feeling that was returned, and always tried to help her; in "Dash to Delaware", they almost end up married. She later has two children by him,ParkerandPiper Perfect. She also has a sister,Pandora Pitstop.

characters.name: Peter Perfect
characters.description: Peter Perfectis a character inWacky Races. He was originally voiced byDaws Butler. He is a racecar driver, driving a drag racer calledThe Turbo Terrific. Peter had a crush onPenelope Pitstop, and often stopped to help her; he sometimes calls her "Pretty Penny". Penelope returned Peter's affections, and in "Dash to Delaware" they nearly end up married. He always stops to say hello to Penelope. He later has two children by her,ParkerandPiper.[1] Sometimes, Peter Perfect demonstrated an unusualelasticityability, although this seemed to be just a gag. Despite numerous mechanical mishaps and other incidents, Peter always keeps a cheerful demeanor and lives up to his image as the perfect gentleman. He is unnaturally strong and is able to lift boulders and even the Double Zero car one-handed.

characters.name: Piper Perfect
characters.description: Piper Perfectis a character inWacky Races Forever. She is the daughter ofPeter PerfectandPenelope Pitstopand sister ofParker Perfect.

characters.name: Professor Pat Pending
characters.description: Professor Pat Pendingis a character onWacky Races.  He was originally voiced byDon Messick.  His name is a pun on the phrase "patent pending". He is a scientist who drives number 3,The Convert-a-Car, a wagon/plane/boat-shaped car equipped with a multitude of gadgets and which can change into just about anything that moves. He's sometimes seen as a rival ofDick DastardlyandMuttley, since his car is equipped with a lot of gadgets, just like the Mean Machine, although they are more defensive, or merely speed-boosting, in nature. He often uses his car's gadgets to help out the other drivers if they all get caught in the same trap, such as when he used his car's de-glue gizmo to free everyone from a glue trap left by Dastardly.

characters.name: Professor Pat Pending

He often uses his car's gadgets to help out the other drivers if they all get caught in the same trap, such as when he used his car's de-glue gizmo to free everyone from a glue trap left by Dastardly.

characters.name: Red Max
characters.description: Red Maxis a character onWacky Races. He was originally voiced byDaws Butler. He is a Bavarian daredevil pilot and air ace whose name is a combination of theRed Baronand theBlue Max. He droveThe Crimson Haybaler, a car/plane hybrid that was capable of limited flight, usually just enough to leapfrog over racers or obstacles in its path. Red Max could use his propeller as a weapon to slice other cars into pieces, which was demonstrated effectively in one race againstThe Ant Hill Mob's long stretchingBulletproof Bomb. The Haybaler also had a mounted machine gun, which was used sporadically. Despite the Haybaler's plane components, its flying ability is often minimal, and Max often has to bail out when the Haybaler breaks down.

characters.description: Rufus RuffcutandSawtoothare characters inWacky Races. Rufus Ruffcut was originally voiced byDaws Butler, while Sawtooth was originally voiced byDon Messick. Rufus is a muscle-bound lumberjack and Sawtooth is a beaver. They drive the number 10 car,The Buzzwagon. Rufus and Sawtooth often build bridges whenever a road is out or missing, or clear a path through dense forests. Rufus Ruffcut:

characters.name: Sergeant Blast and Private Meekley
characters.description: Sergeant Blast and Private Meekley Sergeant BlastandPrivate Meekleyare characters inWacky Races.  Sergeant Blast was originally voiced byDaws Butler, while Private Meekly was originally voiced byPaul Winchell. They are two army soldiers, one a sergeant and the other a private, racing in an army tank/jeep hybrid with a small steamroller‘s wheel attached to the front.

characters.name: Sergeant Blast and Private Meekley
characters.description: Sergeant Blast and Private Meekley Sergeant BlastandPrivate Meekleyare characters inWacky Races.  Sergeant Blast was originally voiced byDaws Butler, while Private Meekly was originally voiced byPaul Winchell. They are two army soldiers, one a sergeant and the other a private, racing in an army tank/jeep hybrid with a small steamroller‘s wheel attached to the front.

characters.name: The Ant Hill Mob
characters.description: TheAnt Hill Mobare characters inWacky RacesandThe Perils of Penelope Pitstop. The Ant Hill Mob are seven gangsters who drive the number seven car,The Bulletproof Bomb.  They are styled after stereotypical early 1900s American gangsters with their car to match.  Their wardrobes are prohibition era fedoras and suits. Clyde's name probably came from car gangster Clyde Barrow while his looks, voice, and mannerisms suggest numerous gangster roles played by Edward G. Robinson.  This is further reinforced in a dialog where he threatens the boys with, "Youse want I should tell Bonnie on you?"[1]an apparent reference to Barrow's accomplice Bonnie Parker.  When broadcast in French, his name was changed to "Al Carbone," a play on Al Capone.  Ring-a-Ding usually queries Clyde's orders or is the one who causes the plan to go wrong in some way.  Clyde also mispronounces his name saying "Ding-a-Ling" instead of "Ring-a-Ding."

characters.name: The Ant Hill Mob
characters.description: TheAnt Hill Mobare characters inWacky RacesandThe Perils of Penelope Pitstop. The Ant Hill Mob are seven gangsters who drive the number seven car,The Bulletproof Bomb.  They are styled after stereotypical early 1900s American gangsters with their car to match.  Their wardrobes are prohibition era fedoras and suits. Clyde's name probably came from car gangster Clyde Barrow while his looks, voice, and mannerisms suggest numerous gangster roles played by Edward G. Robinson.  This is further reinforced in a dialog where he threatens the boys with, "Youse want I should tell Bonnie on you?"[1]an apparent reference to Barrow's accomplice Bonnie Parker.  When broadcast in French, his name was changed to "Al Carbone," a play on Al Capone.  Ring-a-Ding usually queries Clyde's orders or is the one who causes the plan to go wrong in some way.  Clyde also mispronounces his name saying "Ding-a-Ling" instead of "Ring-a-Ding."
